feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Connector
feed.subtitle: Cloud Connector Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Cloud Connector - websocket connection in SAP cloud connector and Hive MQTT
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Business Scenario: Hive MQTT Broker is using 8883 for HTTPS and 8884 ports for WSS. we want to connect our SAP Cloud Connector with Hive MQTT using WebSocket port.  we have some of the question as below:

Question -1 : can we setup connectivity between Hive MQTT broker and SAP CC on WSS?

Question -2: if there is a way to achieve it, can anyone please guide as how we can implement this in our system. 

any help/guidance will be appreciated. 

Feel free to pose any question if you have related to requirement.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: cap application failed while deploying schema to sap hana cloud
entry.content:
I am trying to deploy my DB to SAP HANA CLOUD, But after binding the database connection and deploying getting the following error
The purpose of this task is to deploy schema in SAP HANA PROJECT ( Database explorer )
error:
Error running command _hanaExplorerView.deployFromTree: Deployment failed with code 1, see terminal for more details. This is likely caused by the extension that contributes _hanaExplorerView.deployFromTree.

log:
* Executing task in folder db: echo 'Prepared environment for deployment' && (npm ls --depth 0 --production || npm install) && (node ../db/node_modules/@sap/hdi-deploy/deploy.js "--exit")

Prepared environment for deployment
npm warn config production Use --omit=dev instead.
/home/user/projects/CAP_02/db
└── (empty)

npm error code EJSONPARSE
npm error JSON.parse Failed to parse root package.json
npm error JSON.parse Failed to parse JSON data.
npm error JSON.parse Note: package.json must be actual JSON, not just JavaScript.
npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /home/user/.npm/_logs/2026-02-04T11_28_51_389Z-debug-0.log
npm error code EJSONPARSE
npm error path /home/user/projects/CAP_02/db/package.json
npm error JSON.parse Unexpected end of JSON input while parsing empty string
npm error JSON.parse Failed to parse JSON data.
npm error JSON.parse Note: package.json must be actual JSON, not just JavaScript.
npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /home/user/.npm/_logs/2026-02-04T11_28_51_767Z-debug-0.log

* The terminal process "/bin/bash '-c', 'echo 'Prepared environment for deployment' && (npm ls --depth 0 --production || npm install) && (node ../db/node_modules/@sap/hdi-deploy/deploy.js "--exit")'" terminated with exit code: 1.
* Terminal will be reused by tasks, press any key to close it.

Package.json
{
"name": "CAP_02",
"version": "1.0.0",
"description": "A simple CAP project.",
"dependencies": {
"@cap-js/hana": "^2",
"@sap/cds": "^9",
"@sap/xssec": "^4",
"express": "^4"
},
"scripts": {
"start": "cds-serve"
},
"cds": {
"build": {
"tasks": [
{
"for": "hana",
"dest": "../db"
},
{
"for": "node-cf"
}
]
},
"requires": {
"db": {
"kind": "hana-cloud"
}
}
}
}

entry.title: CAP-consuming on-premise OData API : read ECONNRESET error. 'Error during request to remote serv
entry.content:
Hello, I am not a developer — my background is more functional — but I am trying to build a small study case for my own learning. Please excuse any basic mistakes; I am open to feedback and to deepening my understanding.

My scenario is the following: using a BTP trial account, I am creating an application in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) and connecting it to an SAP S/4HANA system. In this application I also plan to call the OpenAI API.

Currently I am getting the error:

[error] - 502 > {
code: '502',
message: 'Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET'
}

This happens when I call the API API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV from an S/4HANA environment. The S/4 system is a cloud (RISE) environment from an account where I do not have admin access — only SAP GUI access — so I am using SAP Cloud Connector.

What I have already tried:

  1. I changed the backend system — I have access to two different infrastructures — and I get the same error in both.

  2. I cannot set HTTPS in the BTP destination; even with TrustAll = true the BTP destination configuration does not allow it.

  3. I have already reviewed and recreated all my destinations.

  4. I triend, using AI, change my js files, cds and everything  but always fail in the same issue.

 

 

[error] - 502 > {
code: '502',
message: 'Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET'

entry.title: @sap/cap-rfc update to use a RFC websocket destination
entry.content: Will there be an update to the @sap/cap-rfc library to use a destination from the btp destination service, that is configured as RFC Websocket, since the Cloud Connector 2.19 allows such connections? This would make it easier for applications to use secured rfc connections with principal propagation without configuring SNC.

entry.title: 403 Error when calling an on-premise S/4 API from SAP Build Process Automation (SAP Desktop Agent).
entry.content:
We have a BTP Destination configured to point to a customer on prem S/4 system via Cloud Connector.

Our goal is to access S/4 APIs (e.g. API_PRODUCT_SRV) on this system from SAP Build Process Automation via SAP Build Actions.

I tested the action directly and these work fine, but when running the action from within a BPA Automation I get a 403 error.

Both the Action test and the BPA Automation action use the same BTP Destination.

Should this be possible and what are the steps I need to take?

Destination:

Authentication: BasicAuthentication
Proxy Type: OnPremise
URL: http://xxxxxxxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/
Additional Properties:
Key
sap.processautomation.enabled
Value
true
Key
MobileEnabled
Value
true
Key
WebIDEEnabled
Value
true
Key
sap-client
Value
200
Key
WebIDEUsage
Value
odata_abap,dev_abap
Key
sap.build.usage
Value
odata_gen
Key
HTML5.DynamicDestination
Value
true
Key
HTML5.Timeout
Value
600000
Key
AppgyverEnabled
Value
true
 
Key
sap.build.processautomation.enabled
Value
true
Key
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled
Value
true

entry.title: SAP CPI odata invalid csrf token
entry.content:
Hello,

I have an SAP CPI projects and some Iflows. They are grouped into several sub processes.

I have made several odata post requests. But the second odata post Requests shows an csrf invalid error.

I can not find out why.

I have made an:
1) odata get
2) http get fetch CSRF
3) http post using CSRF
4) odata get
5) odata post ← this one fails

 

Do you have any idea how to debug this?

Kind regards,

entry.title: SAP Cloud Connector - Fix for OAuth Token Retrieval on OnPrem Systems
entry.content:
👉👉👉 PLEASE upvote for this solution with this  improvement request 👈👈👈

The Problem

Calling non-SAP APIs thorugh SAP Cloud Connector often fails when Oauth 2.0 tokens are involved. Calling APIs with OAuth authorizaition requires two steps:

  1. Get the OAuth-Token from an Oauth-Token-URL
  2. Calling the actual API using the previously obtained Oauth-Token

Obtaining the OAuth-Token through SAP Cloud Connector works as expected. The token is retrieved and can be used for the subsequent API call. However, the actual API fails stating the token is invalid. The reason for this is as follows:

When calling an Oauth-Token URL through SAP Cloud Connector, it adds the port at the end of the hostname. Example:

Actual Token URL:
https://internal.company-xyz.com/oauth/token

Token URL called by SAP Cloud Connector:
https://internal.company-xyz.com:443/oauth/token

The retrieved JWT-token now includes port 443 in the ISS field as we can see here:

{...
"iss":"https://internal.company-xyz.com:443/oauth/token"
...}


What happens now is that the subsequent API call fails because the API-backend compares the expected string
https://internal.company-xyz.com/oauth/token
against the actual value in the token, which is
https://internal.company-xyz.com:443/oauth/token

One might argue, that this is purely a API-backend problem. Technically, this is correct. Real life looks different though. Most API developers simply implement OAuth verification against the obvious issuer URL in the ISS field and do not even consider to also verify the same URL with a port at the end of the hostname.

The Solution

In case of Oauth-Token-URL system mappings in the SAP Cloud Connector, the HTTPS protocol is chosen. The solution is straight forward: In case HTTPS protocol is chosen, the Internal Port field is no longer mandatory. If left emtpy, no port will be added to the hostname when forwarding the call to the backend system.

Result:
The obtained token will not have the port number after the hostname in the ISS field within the token. Token validation by the API backend system will then be successful. The ISS field in the token now looks like:

{...
"iss":"https://internal.company-xyz.com/oauth/token"
...}

  

👉👉👉 PLEASE upvote for this solution with this  improvement request 👈👈👈

entry.title: Help me create Deep Entity Creation in MDK using S4hana on-premise API.
entry.content: I want to create PR in mobile. I tested api with Header and Items in Postman it worked well. But I can't able to achieve this in MDK. Some Times im facing "Could not find configuration entry for client 100" whenever i try to sync data to backend. Can anyone please me to resolve this. Like how create Purchase Requisition in MDK and Release PR.

entry.title: Configuration Differences When SAP Datasphere Is Integrated into SAP BDC vs. Standalone
entry.content:
Initially, the landscape was as follows:
ECC → SAP LT → SAP Cloud Connector → SAP Datasphere → Azure Data Lake

However, the landscape has now changed to:
ECC → SAP LT → SAP Cloud Connector → SAP Datasphere (integrated into SAP BDC) → Azure Data Lake

Question:
Are there any configuration differences in SAP Cloud Connector, SAP Datasphere (integrated into SAP BDC), and Azure Data Lake compared to the setup when using SAP Datasphere as a standalone system?

entry.title: I cannot connect my Cloud connector to BTP, Invalid server certificate
entry.content:
Hi Experts, I created subaccount in Cloud Connector, but cannot connect to my BTP subaccount, the major error trace log in Cloud connector is as below, could you please advise how to solve it, thanks!

Caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Peer host 'connectivitynotification.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com' does not match certificate DNS names [*.slack.com, slack.com] at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.util.CertificateUtils.assertDNSNames(CertificateUtils.java:269) at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.ssl.SSLHandshakeValidator.assertServerCertificate(SSLHandshakeValidator.java:53) ... 32 common frames omitted 2026-04-14 15:27:52,299 +0800#INFO#com.sap.scc.config#https-jsse-nio2-8443-exec-5# #Change tunnel state to enabled on account c3877adb-7353-458e-b559-ac0a7ce2c1b9[LOC1]@cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com 2026-04-14 15:27:52,303 +0800#INFO#com.sap.scc.rt#https-jsse-nio2-8443-exec-5# #User Administrator starting Tunnel account:///c3877adb-7353-458e-b559-ac0a7ce2c1b9/LOC1 for account USER1 in region cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com 2026-04-14 15:27:59,320 +0800#INFO#com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.client.handshake.ClientProtocolHandshaker#notification-client-171-1# #Sending handshake request for tunnelId: account:///c3877adb-7353-458e-b559-ac0a7ce2c1b9/LOC1 and host: connectivitynotification.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com:443 2026-04-14 15:27:59,456 +0800#ERROR#com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.core.handlers.TunnelClientErrorHandler#notification-client-171-1# #Unexpected problem during tunnel processing in channel [id: 0xd1ba6c24, L:/10.116.30.172:62640 - R:connectivitynotification.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com.slack.com/3.0.66.145:443]; Reason: SSLEngine closed already; Exception type: io.netty.handler.ssl.SslClosedEngineException 2026-04-14 15:27:59,457 +0800#ERROR#com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.client.reconnect.AbstractReconnectableClient#notification-client-171-1# #Connect client to 'connectivitynotification.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com/57.155.80.35:443' failed: Invalid server certificate 2026-04-14 15:27:59,458 +0800#ERROR#com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.core.handlers.TunnelClientErrorHandler#notification-client-171-1# #Unexpected problem during tunnel processing in channel [id: 0xd1ba6c24, L:/10.116.30.172:62640 ! R:connectivitynotification.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com.slack.com/3.0.66.145:443]; Reason: Invalid server certificate; Exception type: com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.ssl.SSLHandshakeException 2026-04-14 15:27:59,461 +0800#WARN#com.sap.scc.config#com.sap.scc.config.AccountConfiguration$$Lambda$450/0x00000001007bfc40# #service channels of account c3877adb-7353-458e-b559-ac0a7ce2c1b9[LOC1]@cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com not restored due to failed tunnel connection 2026-04-14 15:27:59,476 +0800#ERROR#com.sap.scc.rt#https-jsse-nio2-8443-exec-3# #Tunnel account:///c3877adb-7353-458e-b559-ac0a7ce2c1b9/LOC1 connect failed com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: Invalid server certificate Caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Peer host 'connectivitynotification.cf.ap21.hana.ondemand.com' does not match certificate DNS names [*.slack.com, slack.com] at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.util.CertificateUtils.assertDNSNames(CertificateUtils.java:269) at com.sap.core.connectivity.spi.ssl.SSLHandshakeValidator.assertServerCertificate(SSLHandshakeValidator.java:53) ... 32 common frames omitted

entry.title: Build Work Zone: No Content Items Created from S/4HANA FLP Content Exposure (0/0)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently integrating SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition) with an SAP S/4HANA on‑premise system using Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP (Exposure Version 2).

Although the content exposure in S/4HANA seems to be set up correctly, no content items are created in Build Work Zone after channel synchronization.

Observed Behavior

  • The FLP Content Exposure runs successfully in S/4HANA
  • JSON content is generated for:
    • Business Apps
    • Catalogs
    • Groups
    • Roles
  • In Build Work Zone → Channel Manager, the channel status shows Updated
  • However, the Channel Report shows:

    Content Items: 0 / 0
    No content items were created or updated

Expected Behavior

  • Catalogs, apps, and groups exposed from S/4HANA should be converted into content items in Build Work Zone and become available for site usage.

Configuration Details

  • S/4HANA FLP content includes:
    • Business apps (homepage, search)
    • Catalogs assigned to roles
    • Role created specifically for Build Work Zone consumption
  • Content exposure uses Exposure Version 2
  • Channel is created in Build Work Zone in automatic mode
  • Cloud connector and destination setup is in place

Checks Already Done

  • Verified that FLP catalogs are assigned to the role
  • Verified that the role is included in content exposure
  • Channel synchronization completes without errors
  • No errors in BWZ logs, only empty content result

Questions

  1. Which technical roles are mandatory for the S/4HANA user (or technical integration user) to allow Build Work Zone to read FLP content?
  2. Is any additional role required for content item generation?
  3. Are there additional authorization objects (e.g. /UI2/FLP_CONT) that are required?
  4. Are there known prerequisites or limitations when using Exposure Version 2 with Build Work Zone?
  5. Any checks or logs you would recommend to identify why content items are not being generated?

Any guidance or pointers would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Please find the screenshots below for your reference.

Kind regards,
Om Prakash Jha

entry.title: How can S/4HANA Cloud(Public) connect to non-SAP systems in a Private Network for Outboundinterface?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I recently came across S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for the first time, and I would like to ask you because there is a problem with the interface with the non-SAP system located within the private network(no public IP or domain).

---

**Current Situation**

- **Inbound (Legacy System → S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition):** No issues. I have confirmed that our legacy system can reach the Public Edition API endpoints without problems.
- **Outbound (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition → Legacy System):** This is where I'm stuck. Since our legacy systems only have private IP addresses and are not exposed to the internet, I'm unsure how S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can reach them

---

**Questions**

1. **Is SAP Cloud Connector the right approach?**
If so, when configuring Cloud Connector, we can see BTP Subaccount connectivity options — however, we do not see a dedicated Subaccount for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition itself. We can only find the Subaccount associated with our IAS (Identity Authentication Service) tenant.
- Should we use the IAS-associated Subaccount to connect Cloud Connector to BTP?
- Or does S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition have its own dedicated BTP Subaccount that I'm missing?

2. **If number 1 is not the answer, Is SAP Integration Suite (CPI) mandatory for this Outbound scenario, or is it possible to route Outbound calls directly without CPI?**

3. **If number 1 is not the answer, should it be configured with the following architecture**?

```
S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
↕
SAP BTP(IS? CPI?)
↕ (Cloud Connector Tunnel)
Cloud Connector (On-Premise)
↕
non-SAP System (Private Network)
```

In other words, do we need to first connect Cloud Connector to BTP, and then establish a separate connection from BTP to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
If yes, what specific BTP services or configurations are required to bridge between BTP and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Outbound calls?

---

Any guidance, documentation references, or architecture diagrams would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Issue Connecting SAP DMC to MSSQL via CPI (Cloud Foundry) Using Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have integrated SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud (DMC) with our backend SAP S/4HANA system via SAP CPI (Neo). With the new capabilities available in SAP CPI (Cloud Foundry), we are attempting to integrate an on-premise Microsoft SQL Server.

Initially, we tried connecting SAP DMC to the MSSQL server via CPI (CF) by exposing port 1433 publicly, and SAP DMC was able to fetch data from the MSSQL server successfully. However, based on the network team's recommendation, we closed the public access to the MSSQL port.

However, we are now attempting to establish the connection using SAP Cloud Connector, following the relevant SAP blog CPI-MSSQL

In the Cloud Connector (CPI subaccount - Cloud Foundry), we have added the on-premise MSSQL server IP addresses, and the connection status shows as “Reachable” 

In CPI, we have maintained the virtual host and port in the JDBC URL - Ref. screenprint

 

However, we are encountering an error in SAP DMC (please refer to the attached screenshot for the error details).

Error details,

"

{'error" : Root Cause: - Failed to obtain JDBC

Search

Connection; nested exception is
com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The
TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1101 has
failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-
login response. Verify the connection properties and

LabelPrintDM

check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the
host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This
driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.".

Automation Sequences

ClientConnectionld:af429cad-54fc-4b1d-8dc9-
ffe60058025c - The TCP/IP connection to the host

Edg

localhost, port 1101 has failed. Error: "The driver
received an unexpected pre-login response. Verify the
connection properties and check that an instance of

String

SQL Server is running on the host and accepting
TCP/IP connections at the port. This driver can be used
only with SQL Server 2005 or later.".

....

ClientConnectionld:af429cad-54fc-4b1d-8dc9-
ffe60058025c :
org.springframework.jdbc.CannotGetJdbcConnectionE
xception: Failed to obtain JDBC Connection; nested
exception is
com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The
TCP/IP connection to the host localhost, port 1101 has
failed. Error: "The driver received an unexpected pre-
login response. Verify the connection properties and
check that an instance of SQL Server is running on the
host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. This
driver can be used only with SQL Server 2005 or later.".
ClientConnectionld:af429cad-54fc-4b1d-8dc9-
ffe60058025c }"

 

Could you please help us identify the issue and suggest the correct configuration?
 

Advance thanks,

Prabhakar.

.......................................................................................................................................................................................................................................................

The issue was resolved by upgrading the JDBC drivers in CPI to the latest version (mssql-jdbc-12.4.0

entry.title: SAP Cloud Connector, Tomcat related question
entry.content:
Hi, I am new to the SAP products and server support in general. I inherited a server using SAP Cloud Connector. While looking around on the server I realised that there is a tomcat instance running on the server. I have never installed tomcat on this server. Which brings me this question, is there an embedded tomcat in the package install of SAP Cloud Connector? When I asked the past group that was supporting this server and the people who asked for this to be installed on the server, I get 2 different answers.

1. Yes it is embedded in the package install for SAP CC!

2. No, it was installed separately by the people that used to manage the server or someone else.

So, I wanted to ask, which one is true? If tomcat is embedded in the package install of SAP CC, would anyone know the version of tomcat embedded in the most recent version of SAP CC?

entry.title: Connection with Eclipse and Practice System failed
entry.content:
Hi guys,

i want to connect Eclipse with the Practice System but it fails.

Based to the System Guide I only need to copy the URL and paste it to the new abap cloud project.

I clicked on the icon

A page on my browser opened.

But in eclipse I have a error message.

Do you have a idea why?

Thanks

entry.title: SF ECP Connection to Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

currently I'm a bit confused about the correct way to connect an SF ECP system to Datasphere. As far as I could gather from online ressources and the class CL_COS_UTILITIES, SF ECP seems to be a hosted onPrem S4HANA system. So does this mean that if we want to connect it to datasphere we also need a hosted cloud connector/DPA instance? Has anyone done this before and could offer soe guidance? 

Thanks and best regards!

entry.title: Getting 403 error while accessing the On Prem resource though exposed to BTP through cloud connector
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am new to developing application with BTP BAS and cloud connector and recently i was working a React Application (Front-End) which connects to a node js Server (Back-End) that in turn uses Connectivity and Destination service to connect to my On Prem system and fetch data.

 

 

 

I am not using a SAP system as a On Prem rather i am exposing a particular port of linux OS. On this Port, an application is running where it will fetch monitoring data (CPU metrics, OS process etc) and give the response.

 

 

When i ran the backend in BAS (nodemon index.js) and try to access this resource, the response is fine. There is no issue. But while accessing the data in Frontend (which in turn call Backend) to fetch the data, i am getting 403.

From backend

 

From Frontend

 

 

Console log from backend while accessing the resource using Front-End

 

Cloud Connector Trace

 

Summary:

Application Backend -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem Resource is working but Application Frontend -> Application Backend -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem is not working. 

I am using Trial BTP account. Could you please guide / advise me here on what am i doing wrong ?

entry.title: Getting 403 error while accessing the On Prem resource though exposed to BTP through cloud connector
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am new to developing application with BTP BAS and cloud connector and recently i was working a React Application (Front-End) which connects to a node js Server (Back-End) that in turn uses Connectivity and Destination service to connect to my On Prem system and fetch data.

 

I am not using a SAP system as a On Prem rather i am exposing a particular port of linux OS. On this Port, an application is running where it will fetch monitoring data (CPU metrics, OS process etc) and give the response.

 

 

When i ran the backend in BAS (nodemon index.js) and try to access this resource, the response is fine. There is no issue. But while accessing the data in Frontend (which in turn call Backend) to fetch the data, i am getting 403.

From backend

 

From Frontend

 

 

Console log from backend while accessing the resource using Front-End

 

Cloud Connector Trace

 

Summary:

Application Backend -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem Resource is working but Application Frontend -> Application Backend -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem is not working. 

I am using Trial BTP account. Could you please guide / advise me here on what am i doing wrong ?

entry.title: Intermittent 500/XHR Errors While Accessing Custom Node.js On-Prem Service via SAP BTP Cloud Connect
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an intermittent issue while accessing a custom on-premise Node.js service through SAP BTP Connectivity and SAP Cloud Connector. My setup consists of a React frontend running in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), a Node.js backend deployed in BTP, and an on-premise Node.js server exposed through Cloud Connector using a destination with ProxyType=OnPremise. The architecture is basically: React Frontend → BTP Node.js Backend → Connectivity Service → Cloud Connector → On-Prem Node.js Server.

 

 

 

The strange part is that the issue only occurs with my custom Node.js service and not with SAP systems connected through the same Cloud Connector and destination setup. From the BTP backend, I am able to access the on-prem endpoint consistently without any issue using axios calls. However, when the frontend invokes the backend API, the request intermittently fails with XHR 500 errors or messages like “Access denied to resource … ensure to expose the resource correctly in your cloud connector.” Even more confusing is that the failure happens even when the on-prem endpoint only returns a very small plain-text response (~2 KB). So this does not appear to be related to payload size or backend load.

 

 

I have also recently configured a Cloud Connector Location ID and added the same Location ID in the BTP destination configuration. Has anyone faced similar intermittent issues specifically with non-SAP/custom Node.js servers while SAP systems continue to work normally through the same Cloud Connector setup?

Any guidance or troubleshooting suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Connector stuck at "Preparation of tunnel certificate" while connecting BTP Trial
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to connect SAP Cloud Connector to my SAP BTP Trial account.

Cloud Connector installation:
- Master (Primary Installation)
- Running on Windows

Issue:
While adding the subaccount, the status remains at:
"Preparation of tunnel certificate"

Earlier, I also received the message:
"We couldn't find any global accounts associated with your user"

Could anyone help me identify the cause?

Additional Information:
- Cloud Connector Version: 2.19.1
- BTP Trial Account : us10
- Internet connection is working

Thank you.
